data.title: Ascendis Pharma rises amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• Ascendis Pharma (ASND) gained 7.5% amid some takeover speculation.
  • There's talk that a Switzerland-based pharma company and another European firm are eyeing Ascendis (ASND), according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Thursday.
  • Betaville first reported last month that the Danish pharmaceutical firm may have attracted takeover interest.
  • Ascendis (ASND) has a market cap of $13.3 billion.

data.title: Westamerica declares $0.46 dividend
data.description:
• Westamerica (WABC) declared $0.46/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.56%
  • Payable Feb. 13; for shareholders of record Feb. 2; ex-div Feb. 2.
  • See WABC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Polen Focus Growth Fund adds ISRG; exits NFLX and WDAY
data.description:
• Polen Focus Growth Portfolio underperformed with a return of ‑1.4% in Q4 2025, compared with +1.1% for the Russell 1000 Growth Index.
  • During Q4 2025, the fund added a new position in Intuitive Surgical (NASDAQ:ISRG).
  • In Q4 2025, the fund exited Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) and Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY).
  • Source.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase to buy WealthOs to beef up pension technology - report
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) has reportedly agreed to acquire WealthOs, a wealth management software business headed by former Google executive John Herlihy.

The deal was announced to JPMorgan's Nutmeg personal investing division this week, SkyNews reported, citing an internal memo.

The company has been boosting its presence in the U.K., especially in personal banking and wealth management. It started a digital savings bank in the kingdom under the Chase brand in 2021 and acquired Nutmeg, a digital wealth management platform, for ~£700M the same year.

JPMorgan Chase (JPM) stock rose 1.4% in late morning trading on Thursday. 

The company didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Polen Global Growth Portfolio adds to Taiwan Semiconductor, trims stake in Oracle in Q4 2025
data.description:
• Polen Global Growth Composite Portfolio faced relative performance headwinds during the fourth quarter of 2025, with the portfolio returning -2.5% gross of fees against the MSCI All Country World Index's +3.3% return.
  • "Our software holdings were market laggards, especially Oracle, even though virtually all of them are growing their revenue and earnings at or above our expectations," said Polen Global Growth in its fund letter.
  • The fund initiated a new position in Tencent Holdings (TCEHY) (TCTZF) and Spotify Technology (SPOT) in Q4 2025.
  • Polen Global Growth added to its holdings in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) (TSMWF), MercadoLibre (MELI), Siemens Healthineers (SEMHF) (SMMNY), L'Oréal (LRLCF) (LRLCY) (LOR:CA), NVIDIA (NVDA) (NVDA:CA), Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) (GOOG:CA), and Boston Scientific (BSX).
  • To fund these investments and optimize portfolio positioning, the fund sold its stake in Sage Group (SGGEF) (SGPYY), Willis Towers Watson (WTW), ICON (ICLR), and Workday (WDAY).
  • Furthermore, Polen Global Growth trimmed its exposure to Oracle (ORCL), Paycom Software (PAYC), Shopify (SHOP) (SHOP:CA), Adobe (ADBE) (ADBE:CA) (ZADB:CA), Amazon (AMZN), and SAP (SAP) (SAPGF).
  • "The majority of Global Growth's exposure intentionally resides in sectors, industries, and companies outside of the Gen AI and datacenter capex themes, and that we believe can perform well regardless of underlying market driver," said the fund letter.
  • Source: Fund Letter

data.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX lines up four banks for IPO - FT
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX has lined up four investment banks for leading roles on its initial public offering.Bank of America (BAC), Goldman Sachs (GS), JPMorgan (JPM), and Morgan Stanley (MS), are being lined up for senior roles leading the IPO,  according to a  Financial Times report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

SpaceX executives have had meetings in recent weeks with banks as the rocket maker prepares for an IPO as soon as this year, according to the report. 

Other banks are also expected to get roles in the SpaceX IPO, the FT said. No final decisions have been made, and the situation could change.

The banks declined to comment to the FT. SpaceX didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

SpaceX aims to raise over $25 billion in 2026 to fund orbital AI data centers and accelerate Starship development. CFO Bret Johnsen  announced IPO preparations in December 2025, with Musk targeting completion by July 2026.

data.title: Procter & Gamble turns higher after saying its worst quarter is behind it
data.description:
Procter & Gamble (PG) turned around a premarket loss after CFO Andre Schulten stated on the company's earnings call that the household products giant has completed what it fully expects will be the softest quarter of the fiscal year.

On Wall Street, the general vibe of analysts is optimistic that the Cincinnati-based company can see improved results going forward.

BNP Paribas analyst Kevin Gundy  said that despite Procter & Gamble's (PG) muted top-line results and sentiment increasingly tethered to sequential improvement in P&G’s volume performance, the firm sees EPS guidance achievability as high. 

Morgan Stanley analyst Dara Mohsenian highlighted that P&G's (PG) FQ2 results were weak on a year-over-year basis, but the bar was low, and there is room for clear sequential organic sales improvement in the second half of the fiscal year. "Negative FY EPS revisions should be limited with relatively in-line margin results despite topline weakness. US performance will be key going forward, with easier comparisons and a greater innovation pipeline in key areas, but US promotion is picking back up in the HPC sector, and PG still has struggles in some key areas (baby)," wrote Mohsenian.

Evercore ISI analyst Robert Ottenstein pointed to P&G's (PG) declaration that it has confidence in a strong second half as a positive. 

Shares of Procter & Gamble (PG) were up 2.6% at 11:22 a.m. to trade at their highest level of 2026.

data.title: Sphere owner's shares rally after snagging BTIG's bullish rating
data.description:
BTIG upgraded Sphere Entertainment Co. (SPHR), the owner and operator of the famous Las Vegas Sphere, to “buy” from a previous investment rating of “neutral” on Thursday.

Shares of the company are up nearly 6% before midday.

The research firm said it now sees multiple catalysts, such as incremental U.S. and international Spheres, premium content licensing similar to The Wizard of Oz, incremental sponsorship deals, and the addition of third-party concerts, among others, to potentially drive stock upside.

"While we have always been clear on improving fundamentals, profitability, and narrative, we underappreciated execution ability under backdrop of a weakening lower-end consumer (target customer really premium) and therefore rerating potential," the research firm said in its note.

BTIG expects profitability for the company to improve into 2026 and 2027. It also thinks the expansion opportunity is appealing both in the U.S. and internationally, with content licensing offering a potentially higher-margin revenue channel.

SPHR now has a price target of $110, implying an upside of 18.1%.

data.title: Lawmakers urge Treasury to probe AI infrastructure debt risks
data.description:
U.S. Democratic Senators Elizabeth Warren, Richard Blumenthal, Chris Van Hollen and Tina Smith on Thursday asked the U.S. Treasury Department's Financial Stability Oversight Council to start a formal investigation into big tech firms' growing reliance on large, private, often “opaque” debt financings to support the buildout of AI infrastructure.

“AI companies unable to rapidly increase revenues and service their massive debt loads could cause destabilizing losses for an interconnected set of financial institutions, triggering a broader financial crisis that harms the economy,” the lawmakers wrote in a letter to the FSOC, Bloomberg reported. 

The senators pointed to a $30B financing deal involving Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) and Pacific Investment Management Co. to fund Meta Platforms' (META) Hyperion data center site, structured via a special-purpose vehicle that keeps the associated debt off Meta's balance sheet.

Such an off-balance-sheet structure “conceals the company’s true financial condition, allowing it to appear healthier and less leveraged than it actually is and enabling it to borrow more than they otherwise could,” the letter said.

Because the obligations are effectively backed by the parent tech firm, the debt is typically assigned an investment-grade rating.

The four senators requested a response from the FSOC by Feb. 13, Bloomberg reported.

The Treasury Department did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Essity AB (publ) GAAP EPS of SEK4.69, revenue of SEK34.69M
data.description:
• Essity AB (publ) press release (ETTYF): Q4 GAAP EPS of SEK4.69.
  • Revenue of SEK34.69M.
  • Organic sales growth amounted to -1.1%, of which volume accounted for -0.2% and price/mix -0.9%
  • EBITA increased 9% to SEK 5,005m (4,585)
  • EBITA excl. IAC increased 3% to SEK 5,117m (4,969). Excl. currency translation effects, EBITA excl. IAC increased 12%, corresponding to SEK 594m.
  • EBITA margin excl. IAC increased 1.6 percentage points to 14.7% (13.1)
  • ROCE increased to 17.6% (15.8) and ROCE excl. IAC increased to 18.0% (17.1)
  • Profit for the period, total operations, increased to SEK 3,224m (2,893)
